metadata.name: Full-Funnel Conversion Auditor
metadata.description: Audits the complete customer acquisition funnel from first awareness touchpoint through landing page to post-conversion experience. Maps message continuity, emotional arc, promise escalation, visual consistency, trust accumulation, and friction across every stage. Scores against a 10-dimension funnel continuity rubric (20 points) and produces a stage-by-stage improvement plan with specific fixes at each handoff.
metadata.author: homer@amiara.ai

questions.question: What best describes your primary customer acquisition funnel?
options: Paid search (Google/Bing ads → landing page → form/purchase)
Paid social (Facebook/Instagram/TikTok/LinkedIn ads → landing page → form/purchase)
Email nurture (email sequence → landing page → form/purchase)
Retargeting (previous visit → retargeting ad → landing page → form/purchase)
Referral/affiliate (partner/friend → referral link → landing page → form/purchase)
Social organic (social posts → bio link/landing page → form/purchase)

questions.question: What is the URL of the main landing page in this funnel?
What is the ultimate conversion action for this funnel?
Describe the person this funnel is built for. Who are they, what's their biggest pain, and what outcome do they want?
questions.hint: E.g., 'SaaS founders doing $1-5M ARR who are overwhelmed by marketing and want a repeatable growth engine without hiring a CMO'

questions.question: What channels does this funnel use to reach new people? (select all that apply)
Which single channel drives the most volume or revenue?
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'Facebook/Instagram ads — about 60% of our traffic'

questions.question: Paste or describe the awareness creative for your primary channel. (ad copy, email subject + body, social post, search ad text — whatever starts the journey)
questions.placeholder: Paste the full ad copy, email, or post text here. If it's a video or image ad, describe what's shown and any text overlay.

questions.question: In one sentence, what does this creative promise the viewer?
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'Save 10 hours per week on marketing with AI-powered content', 'Get 30% off your first order this weekend'

questions.question: What is the exact CTA text or link that gets them to the landing page?
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'Learn More', 'Shop Now', 'Get Your Free Guide', the email button text, the link anchor text, etc.

questions.question: What has the person seen or read BEFORE they click? Describe the full context.
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'They've seen a 15-second video ad showing our product in action with the headline "Stop guessing. Start growing." The ad has a customer quote overlay. The CTA button says Learn More.'

questions.question: What does the person expect to find when they land on the page?
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'They expect to see pricing and features for the AI marketing tool', 'They expect to see the 30% off offer with a way to shop immediately'

questions.question: Walk us through exactly what happens after someone clicks the main CTA on the landing page. Every step, page, and field.
questions.placeholder:
E.g., 'They click "Start Free Trial" → see a form asking for name, email, company name, company size → click Submit → redirected to a Calendly page to book an onboarding call → get a confirmation email'

Or: 'They click "Add to Cart" → cart page shows item + shipping estimate → checkout asks for email, shipping address, payment → order confirmation page → confirmation email with tracking'

questions.question: URL of the conversion page or form, if different from the landing page (optional)
If there's a form, list every field (in order)
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'First name (required), Email (required), Phone (optional), Company (required), Company size dropdown (required), Message textarea (optional)'

questions.question: If there's a payment step, describe it. Pricing, payment methods, any surprise fees or upsells.
questions.placeholder: E.g., '$49/month billed annually, credit card only, no free trial, 30-day money-back guarantee shown on checkout page'

questions.question: What does the person see/receive immediately after converting? (thank-you page, confirmation email, redirect, etc.)
questions.placeholder:
E.g., 'Redirected to a thank-you page that says "You're in! Check your email for next steps." Then they get an automated email with a link to create their account.'

Or: 'Order confirmation page with order number + estimated delivery. Confirmation email sent immediately with tracking link.'

questions.question: What follow-up do they receive in the first 7 days? (emails, calls, onboarding, etc.)
questions.placeholder:
E.g., 'Day 0: Welcome email with login link. Day 1: Onboarding email with setup guide. Day 3: Check-in email asking if they need help. Day 7: Feature highlight email.'

Or: 'Day 0: Confirmation email. Day 2: Shipping notification. Day 5: Delivered notification. Day 7: Review request email.'

questions.question: How quickly does the customer get their first 'win' or value from what they purchased/signed up for?
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'They can create their first AI-generated blog post within 5 minutes of logging in', 'Product arrives in 3-5 days', 'Demo call is usually within 24 hours of booking'

questions.question: Share any conversion data you have for this funnel. (optional but very helpful)
questions.placeholder:
E.g., 'Ad CTR: 1.8%, Landing page conversion: 3.2%, Form completion: 45%, Email open rate: 38%'

Or: 'We get about 5,000 visitors/month from Facebook ads. 150 fill out the form. 30 book a call. 8 become customers.'

questions.question: Where do you think people are dropping off, and why?
questions.placeholder:
E.g., 'Bounce rate on the landing page is 68%. We think the page doesn't match the ad well enough.'

Or: 'Lots of people fill out the form but never show up for the demo call.'

questions.question: What have you already tried to improve this funnel? (optional)
questions.placeholder: E.g., 'We A/B tested 3 headlines. We shortened the form from 6 fields to 4. We added testimonials to the landing page.'

searches.query_template: brand guidelines visual identity voice tone
customer profile ideal customer persona buying behavior

instructions.system_prompt:
You are a full-funnel conversion strategist. Your job is NOT to analyze a landing page in isolation — it's to audit the entire customer acquisition journey from first awareness touchpoint through landing page to post-conversion experience, and identify where the funnel breaks.

The user has described their funnel. You will map it, audit every stage and every handoff, score it against the funnel-continuity-audit skill's 10-dimension rubric, and produce a stage-by-stage improvement plan.

## Your Core Principle

Every click is a micro-contract. The awareness creative makes a promise. The click-through CTA accepts the promise. The landing page must deliver on it. The conversion action must honor it. The post-conversion experience must fulfill it. When any stage breaks this chain, conversions die.

## What Makes This Different From a Landing Page Audit

The other agents in the LP suite audit the landing page itself. You audit the JOURNEY. Your analysis is about the connections between stages:
- Does the ad's promise match the page's headline?
- Does the page's emotional arc match what the ad set up?
- Does the post-click experience deliver what the CTA promised?
- Does the post-conversion follow-up reinforce the original promise?
- Is trust building incrementally across stages or resetting?
- Is the visitor being asked for proportional commitment at each stage?

## Funnel Type Adaptation

The user selected funnel type: {{responses.funnel.type}}.

Adapt your audit priorities:
- **Paid search:** Message match between search query intent → ad copy → landing page is the #1 priority. Search visitors have high intent — the funnel should get out of the way.
- **Paid social:** The ad is an interrupt. The landing page must re-hook attention. Social funnels need MORE persuasion on the page because interest is shallow.
- **Email nurture:** Subject line → email body → landing page continuity is critical. Warm visitors expect acknowledgment of their relationship.
- **Organic/SEO:** Content-to-conversion handoff is the key audit point. Often there's an extra stage (blog/content → CTA → landing page).
- **Retargeting:** Visitors have prior context. Does the funnel acknowledge this? "Welcome back" messaging matters.
- **Referral:** Visitors trust the referrer, not you. Does the funnel leverage the referrer's endorsement?
- **Multi-touch:** Audit the full sequence. Identify where the handoffs between touches break down.

## Cross-Reference With LP Suite

Check KB for existing analyses from the LP suite (scorecard, headline, ICP, layout, CTA, social proof, mobile). If found, incorporate their findings into the landing page stage of your funnel audit. Don't re-analyze what's already been analyzed — build on it.

## Output Requirements

Be specific and actionable at every stage. Don't say "improve message match" — show the exact ad copy, the exact page headline, and write the exact revised copy that would fix the mismatch. Don't say "reduce friction" — list the exact fields to remove and the exact steps to eliminate.

processing_steps.description: Pull business context, check for existing LP suite analyses, and synthesize all user inputs into a funnel map
Crawl the landing page, audit every accessible stage, and score the full funnel against the 10-dimension rubric
processing_steps.prompt:
## Funnel Map & Context
{{prior_steps.gather-context-and-upstream.content}}

## User Inputs (for reference)
- **Funnel type:** {{responses.funnel.type}}
- **Landing page URL:** {{responses.funnel.lp_url}}
- **Conversion goal:** {{responses.funnel.conversion_goal}}
- **ICP:** {{responses.funnel.icp}}
- **Awareness creative:** {{responses.funnel.awareness_creative}}
- **Awareness promise:** {{responses.funnel.awareness_promise}}
- **Click trigger:** {{responses.funnel.click_trigger}}
- **Click expectations:** {{responses.funnel.click_expectations}}
- **Conversion steps:** {{responses.funnel.conversion_steps}}
- **Form fields:** {{responses.funnel.form_fields}}
- **Payment details:** {{responses.funnel.payment_details}}
- **Thank-you experience:** {{responses.funnel.thank_you}}
- **Follow-up sequence:** {{responses.funnel.follow_up}}
- **First value moment:** {{responses.funnel.first_value}}
- **Metrics:** {{responses.funnel.metrics}}
- **Known issues:** {{responses.funnel.known_issues}}

## Instructions

### Part 1: Crawl Accessible Touchpoints

Use search_web to crawl:
1. **Landing page** at {{responses.funnel.lp_url}} — extract full page structure, headline, subheadline, all CTAs, social proof, offer details, forms, navigation
2. **Conversion page** at {{responses.funnel.conversion_url}} (if provided and different from LP) — extract form fields, payment flow, trust signals
3. **Competitor funnels** — search for 2-3 competitors and examine their awareness → conversion journey

### Part 2: Stage-by-Stage Audit

For EACH of the 5 stages, produce a detailed audit:

#### Stage 1: Awareness Audit
- **Creative analysis:** Evaluate the awareness creative the user provided. What promise does it make? How specific is it? Does it target the right ICP?
- **Channel-ICP fit:** Is the primary channel appropriate for reaching this ICP?
- **Hook quality:** Would this creative stop the scroll / earn the open / earn the click in a competitive environment?
- **Promise clarity:** Is the promise specific enough that the visitor knows what they'll find if they click?

#### Stage 2: Click-Through Audit
- **CTA-to-expectation alignment:** Does the CTA ("{{responses.funnel.click_trigger}}") set accurate expectations for the landing page?
- **Commitment calibration:** Is the CTA's implied commitment level appropriate for the relationship stage?
- **Promise escalation:** Does the click-through add new value or information beyond the awareness stage?

#### HANDOFF 1→2→3: The Critical Transition
This is where most funnels leak. Analyze in detail:
- **Message match:** Place the awareness promise, the CTA text, and the landing page headline side by side. Do they tell the same story?
- **Visual continuity:** Does the landing page's look and feel match the awareness creative?
- **Emotional continuity:** Does the page's energy match what the creative set up?
- **Speed to promise:** How quickly does the landing page deliver on the promise? Is it in the headline, or buried below the fold?

#### Stage 3: Landing Page Audit
- **Incorporate LP suite findings** if available from KB (scorecard, headline, ICP, layout, CTA, social proof, mobile). Summarize their scores and key findings.
- **If no prior analyses exist,** evaluate the key landing page elements: headline, subheadline, CTAs, social proof, offer clarity, mobile experience.
- **Funnel-specific evaluation:** Does the page work as a CONTINUATION of the journey, not as a standalone page? Is it aware of where the visitor came from?

#### HANDOFF 3→4: CTA to Conversion
- **What happens when they click the CTA?** Same page scroll? New page? Modal? Redirect to third-party?
- **Surprise audit:** Is there ANYTHING in the conversion flow that the landing page didn't prepare them for? (Extra fields, pricing they didn't see, a login requirement, a redirect to a different-looking page)
- **Friction inventory:** Count every click, field, page load, and decision between CTA click and completion.

#### Stage 4: Conversion Action Audit
- **Form analysis:** Evaluate every field listed in {{responses.funnel.form_fields}}. Which are necessary? Which create friction? Which could be removed or deferred?
- **Payment flow:** If there's payment, evaluate surprise fees, value reinforcement at checkout, trust signals, risk reversal visibility.
- **Cognitive load:** Does the conversion process introduce new information or decisions?
- **Mobile readiness:** How does this conversion flow work on mobile?

#### HANDOFF 4→5: Conversion to Delivery
- **Confirmation quality:** Is the thank-you experience immediate, clear, and reassuring?
- **Promise bridge:** Does the post-conversion experience connect back to the original awareness promise?
- **Momentum maintenance:** Is there energy and direction after conversion, or does it feel like hitting a wall?

#### Stage 5: Post-Conversion Audit
- **Thank-you page:** Does it reinforce the value of what they just did? Does it set expectations for what happens next?
- **Follow-up sequence:** Is the follow-up timely, relevant, and building toward the first value moment?
- **Time to first value:** How long until they actually EXPERIENCE the value that was promised in Stage 1? Is this acceptable for the commitment they made?
- **Buyer's remorse prevention:** Is there anything in the first 24-48 hours that validates their decision?
- **Relationship path:** Is there a clear next step in the relationship? (Onboarding → usage → upgrade → referral)

### Part 3: Score the Funnel

Using the funnel-continuity-audit skill's 10-dimension rubric, score the entire funnel:

| # | Dimension | Score (0-2) | Key Evidence |
|---|-----------|-------------|---------------|
| 1 | Message continuity | | [cite specific copy from each stage] |
| 2 | Emotional arc | | [describe emotion at each stage] |
| 3 | Promise escalation | | [list what's asked vs delivered at each stage] |
| 4 | Visual/tonal continuity | | [describe brand feel at each stage] |
| 5 | Trust accumulation | | [list trust signals at each stage] |
| 6 | ICP consistency | | [note language/targeting shifts] |
| 7 | Friction management | | [count clicks/fields/steps] |
| 8 | Handoff quality | | [describe each transition] |
| 9 | Objection resolution timing | | [map objections to where they're addressed] |
| 10 | Post-conversion follow-through | | [describe post-conversion experience] |

**Total funnel continuity score: ___ / 20**

### Part 4: Identify the Leakiest Handoff

Based on the scoring, conversion data (if provided), and your analysis, identify the single biggest conversion leak in the funnel. This is the #1 priority fix.

Explain:
- Which handoff is leaking
- What specifically is breaking
- Estimated conversion impact
- The specific fix (with exact copy, exact changes)

### Part 5: Stage-by-Stage Recommendations

For EACH stage with a problem, provide:

#### Stage [N]: [Problem Title]
- **Current state:** [quote exact copy/describe exact experience]
- **Problem:** [what's wrong and why it matters]
- **Fix:** [exact replacement copy, exact change, exact addition]
- **Downstream impact:** [how this fix improves subsequent stages]
- **Priority:** High / Medium / Low
- **Effort:** Quick (< 1 hour) / Medium (1-4 hours) / Significant (4+ hours)

Rank all recommendations by conversion impact.

### Part 6: Funnel Map Artifact

Create a comprehensive funnel map showing:
- Each stage with its current state
- Scores for each dimension
- Leak points with severity
- Recommended fixes at each stage
- The promise chain (current vs recommended)

Use create_document_artifact to save this as a visual reference.

### Part 7: Save to KB

Save the complete funnel audit to KB using add_file_to_kb with title: 'Funnel Audit: [business name] — [funnel type] — [date]'.

Include a YAML header with:
```
type: funnel-continuity-audit
funnel_type: {{responses.funnel.type}}
research_date: [today's date]
landing_page_url: {{responses.funnel.lp_url}}
conversion_goal: {{responses.funnel.conversion_goal}}
funnel_score: [X/20]
leakiest_handoff: [stage N → stage N+1]
primary_channel: [channel]
```

version_history.changes: Initial version — full-funnel conversion audit covering awareness through post-conversion, 10-dimension rubric, stage-by-stage questions mapping the complete customer journey, cross-references all LP suite outputs from KB
version_history.author: homer@amiara.ai
